angle_summary: Petals has built one of the strongest local reputations in Westport, with 312 reviews at 4.8 stars, but the current website is not structured to convert that trust into new bookings from organic search. Targeted improvements to navigation, on-page SEO, and content would help the site work harder for new visitors who have not yet discovered the salon through word of mouth.
proof_points: The desktop PageSpeed score of 25 is notably weak and below the mobile score of 52, suggesting rendering or resource loading issues that may be affecting how Google assesses the site's quality signals.
The contact form exists but is difficult to find, creating friction in the enquiry pathway at a point where new visitors are deciding whether to book or move on.

With no blog or content section present, the site is missing an opportunity to rank for local and service-specific search queries in the Westport and Mayo area where new clients are actively looking for a salon.
recommended_offer_type: Local SEO starter (schema, pages, content plan)
offer_scope_points: On-page SEO review and optimisation of page titles, meta descriptions, and heading structure across key service pages targeting queries such as hairdresser Westport and hair salon Mayo.
Implementation of local business schema markup and a dedicated location or area page to strengthen geographic relevance signals for Westport and surrounding areas.
A simple content plan outlining three to six blog or update topics relevant to Petals' services and the local Westport audience, designed to be low-maintenance and build organic visibility over time.
personalisation_notes: Petals has 312 Google reviews at 4.8 stars, which is an unusually strong local reputation for a salon in a town the size of Westport and gives the outreach a genuine, specific compliment to open with.

The site is built on Phorest, a managed platform with known SEO and design limitations, so any proposal should acknowledge this upfront and frame the offer around what is achievable within or alongside the existing setup.
suggested_cta: Would you be open to a short call this week so I can walk you through a few specific things I noticed and show you what a focused set of improvements could look like for Petals?
avoid_saying: Avoid saying the website looks unprofessional or is letting the business down, as the owner may have invested in the Phorest setup and be proud of what they have built.
Avoid implying that Phorest is the wrong choice or suggesting a full platform switch without first understanding how much control the owner has and whether they are open to that conversation.
unknowns_to_confirm: How much design and SEO control does the owner currently have within their Phorest plan, and are they open to a custom front-end or hybrid solution if platform limitations are confirmed?

Who currently manages the website, is it handled in-house, through Phorest support, or via an existing agency relationship, as this will affect how the proposal is framed and who the decision-maker is.
